candidates.name: Auto-routing via type detection
candidates.description: partition() auto-detects file type via libmagic and routes to type-specific partitioner. T28 needs equivalent: detect source type (URL, repo, file) and route to right extractor

candidates.name: Self-describing FileType registry
candidates.description: Enum where each member declares partitioner path, deps, extras, MIME types, extensions. Adding a format = adding one enum member. T28 source type registration pattern

candidates.name: Strategy fallback chain
candidates.description: AUTO→HI_RES→OCR_ONLY→FAST with dependency checking. Maps to T28 Quick/Standard/Deep with graceful degradation when tools unavailable

candidates.name: Graceful dependency checking
candidates.description: dependency_exists() checks at runtime, logs warning, falls back. T28 needs this for optional deps (Whisper, Chrome, gh CLI)

candidates.name: Golden-file snapshot testing
candidates.description: 40+ connector types tested via expected markdown output comparison. Model for T28 extraction regression testing

candidates.name: Element type hierarchy with metadata
candidates.description: Dataclass elements with coordinates, data source provenance, permissions. Informs T28 extraction output schema design

candidates.name: Chunking with table isolation
candidates.description: Tables never merge with adjacent text during chunking. Title-based semantic boundaries. Relevant for T28 analysis layer

candidates.name: Text cleaning pipeline
candidates.description: Bullet normalization, ligature replacement, whitespace cleanup. Reminder that extraction output needs post-processing normalization

candidates.name: S3-backed performance benchmarking
candidates.description: Benchmark results tagged with architecture/instance/git hash. Production-grade perf tracking model

candidates.name: unstructured-ingest connector ecosystem
candidates.description: 40+ source connectors (Salesforce, Notion, Confluence, Jira, Slack, Discord, S3, etc.). T28 connector layer reference

candidates.name: Don't replicate ontology V2 over-engineering
candidates.description: Pydantic HTML intermediate representation adds complexity without value. Team acknowledges it. T28 should go extraction→typed output directly

candidates.name: Don't build per-format parsers in TypeScript
candidates.description: 30+ partitioners represent years of edge cases. T28 should delegate parsing to existing tools and focus on orchestration

candidates.name: Don't fragment extraction and analysis across repos
candidates.description: Embedding moved to unstructured-ingest, fragmenting DX. Keep T28 extraction→analysis→synthesis as single coherent system

cross_repo_connections.detail: Bedrock uses AWS-managed extraction (Transcribe, Textract). Unstructured provides open-source alternative with same multi-format goal but different execution model (local vs cloud)
Bulk-transcribe uses Whisper for audio. Unstructured has partition_audio() that also wraps Whisper. Different scopes but shared audio extraction pattern
youtube-transcript-api extracts captions via API (no ML). Unstructured doesn't handle YouTube at all — these cover different source types
Both target multi-format document extraction with LLM-ready output. Direct competitor — queued as Cluster A repo #2 in T28 plan
Reader converts URLs to LLM-friendly text. Overlaps with unstructured's HTML partitioner but adds URL-first approach. Cluster A repo #3
